text:
Once upon a time, in a vibrant little village surrounded by woods, there lived a girl named Melodie. Melodie loved listening to the rustling leaves and distant birds. Every evening, she'd wander into the forest to count the owls. She never counted them out loud, not even to herself; that seemed silly.

She knew exactly where the owls liked to perch. There was one hollowed out log near the elderflower hedge that had hosted several owls throughout the year.

One particular evening, after a day buzzing around ponds collecting colorful lintels, Melodie felt an unusual sensation. It wasn't fear; it was curiosity. She made her way slowly toward the log.

What she found was astonishing. Two owls - a beautiful snowy owl with a tufted crown and great obsidian eyes, and a magnificent fluffy owl with feathers like sapphire flecks.

"You!" exclaimed the snowy owl. "My lady owl. How delightful!"

"I'm... Melodie," she
